examples: For administrative purposes, it was very inconvenient to have years of different lengths.
People still found coins inconvenient to carry, when they wanted to buy something expensive.
"It's no disgrace to be colored," the black entertainer Bert Williams famously observed early in the century, "but it is awfully inconvenient."
We think Al Gore would agree - it is an inconvenient truth that can no longer be ignored.
An inconvenient truth, not adequately addressed by Al Gore in his movie, is that environmentalism makes life complicated.
